mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) mechanically raises aggregate wage income and consumption demand, which translates into higher expected corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward return following positive month-over-month PAYEMS growth is non-positive, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the federal funds rate above 0.25 percent signals the end of zero-interest-rate policy, raising the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and triggering negative 60-day S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Market pricing for Fed liftoff in 2015
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS is above 0.25%, or the mean return is not statistically different from zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in real retail sales (RSAFS) and decreases in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signal a demand expansion coupled with labor market tightening, jointly amplifying earnings expectations and producing positive 20-day S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS growth and UNRATE changes does not significantly predict 20-day returns (t-stat < 2.0), or returns in the joint-signal quadrant are lower than in single-series quadrants.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth (INDPRO) generates positive S&P 500 returns only when unemployment exceeds 6 percent, because high labor slack allows output expansion without wage-driven margin compression; when UNRATE is above this threshold, INDPRO accelerations predict positive 60-day forward returns.
failure_condition: In periods with UNRATE above 6.0%, positive INDPRO changes fail to produce positive mean 60-day forward returns, or the conditional Sharpe ratio is negative.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) larger than 0.3 percent signals an inflation surprise that reduces real equity earnings multiples and triggers immediate risk-off repositioning, producing negative 5-day S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 does not produce negative 5-day returns following CPIAUCSL spikes greater than 0.3%, or the hit rate of negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects strengthening manufacturing momentum that drives positive earnings revisions and positive 20-day S&P 500 forward returns as production surprises feed into analyst estimates.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month INDPRO changes fail to predict positive 20-day returns with Rank IC above 0.03 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) accelerations amplify construction and durable goods demand, and this effect on equity returns is magnified when monetary policy is highly accommodative (FEDFUNDS below 0.5 percent); the interaction predicts positive 60-day S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal of HOUST acceleration and FEDFUNDS below 0.5% produces negative mean 60-day returns, or the interaction term is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in a forward-return regression.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) greater than 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating household income trajectories and triggers immediate equity de-risking, producing negative 5-day S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 50% of instances when UNRATE rises more than 0.2pp, or the mean return is positive.
